name: Veal Chops with Anchovy and Caper Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil in a heavy skillet large enough to accommodate chops. Pat chops dry and coat with flour, shaking off excess. Sauté chops in very hot oil, turning once.
2. Meanwhile, melt butter in a small skillet and sauté ham over medium heat for 1 minute. Add anchovies and capers, mashing ingredients down with a fork to make a paste.
3. When chops are done (about 8 to 10 minutes), remove them to a heated ovenproof platter and keep warm in a 200-degree oven with a piece of aluminum foil placed loosely over the top.
4. Pour off excess oil from skillet used for cooking chops. Add brandy, scraping up browned bits. Reduce liquid by half by boiling.
5. Pour into ham and anchovy mixture. Add whipping cream, heating and stirring while it blends.
6. Pour sauce over chops and garnish with parsley. Serves 6.
